text:
Market today: Cautious optimism on inflation, with Fed officials signaling a peak and wholesale prices declining. This generally supports your tech/chip names by easing rate pressure. Geopolitical tensions with Iran, however, add uncertainty.

Your holdings: SOXL (-34.84%) and SPAL (-35.98%) are flagged "SELL"/"NEW" with significant losses. These leveraged ETFs are volatile and decay, and margin amplifies drops. Other holdings are fine, though SMH and PTNM are down.

Watch: Fed Chairman Warsh's testimony and any escalation in the Iran situation.

Bottom line: Focus on managing leveraged positions and stay vigilant on macro and geopolitical developments.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming this "SELL" flagged, leveraged position given its -34.84% loss and decay. Re-evaluate your risk management.
SPAL: Review your new, leveraged SPAL position, down -35.98%. Its volatility and decay are amplified by margin; adjust stop accordingly.
TXN: With cooling inflation supporting chips, consider a new buy on TXN at 300.34-300.04, stop 299.38, per scanner.
